title: Optics-microwave entanglement and state teleportation mediated by a cavity magnomechanical system
authors: F. Engelhardt, A. V. Bondarenko, A. Metelmann, Ya. M. Blanter, S. Viola Kusminskiy, V. A. S. V. Bittencourt
abstract: Generating usable output-entanglement in continuous variable systems can serve as a viable resource for improving applications in quantum information science. In this work, we show how to generate steady-state output-entanglement in a two-stage conversion setup between optical and microwave photon which employs resonantly coupled magnetic and mechanical excitations, as proposed in Phys. Rev. Applied 18, 044059 (2022). We show that the entanglement can be maximized for the same set of parameters which optimize the frequency-conversion efficiency, and that it can be leveraged for a teleportation-based state-transfer protocol for coherent input-states with fidelity close to unity. We propose an implementation based on an Yittrium Iron Garnet disk of micrometer scale, and use both simulation results and reasonable estimates to assess the performance under optimized conditions. We find a maximum teleportation fidelity of 0.75 for the proposed setup.
summary: This paper proposes a two-stage magnomechanical converter to generate entanglement between optical and microwave photons. By using a YIG disk as a mediator, the system enables high-fidelity quantum state teleportation. The study demonstrates that this setup overcomes the efficiency bottlenecks found in single-stage frequency conversion models.
main_problem: Generating and distributing steady-state entanglement between optical and microwave photons to enable high-fidelity quantum state teleportation across vastly different frequency regimes.
main_result: The proposed two-stage magnomechanical converter can achieve a maximum teleportation fidelity of approximately 0.75 for coherent states and generates significant output entanglement without requiring matched cooperativities.
method: The study uses Heisenberg-Langevin equations and a covariance matrix formalism to quantify entanglement (logarithmic negativity) and teleportation fidelity (VBK protocol) within a Gaussian framework.
model_or_system: A hybrid magnomechanical system consisting of a micrometer-scale Yttrium Iron Garnet (YIG) disk coupled to an optical fiber and a microwave ring resonator, involving coupled optical, mechanical, magnon, and microwave modes.
key_observables: Logarithmic negativity (E_N), teleportation fidelity (F), and quantum steering (S).
important_parameters: Optomechanical and magnomechanical cooperativities, magnon frequency, optical pump enhancement, and thermal occupation (temperature).
main_assumptions: The system is treated as a linearized Gaussian system; it assumes a blue-detuned driving scheme and neglects certain higher-order nonlinearities and optomagnonic couplings.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the device geometry; Figure 4 illustrates entanglement vs. magnon frequency; Figure 6 plots fidelity and entanglement against temperature and coupling efficiency; Figure 11 shows quantum steering alongside entanglement; Figure 12 displays spatial mode profiles.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the physical problem of frequency conversion, describes the hybrid magnomechanical setup and Hamiltonian, details the mathematical framework for entanglement and teleportation, presents numerical results for performance and stability, and concludes with a discussion of experimental feasibility.
why_interesting: It addresses a fundamental challenge in quantum networking: bridging the gap between optical-frequency flying qubits and microwave-frequency stationary qubits using a hybrid bosonic mediator.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

FIG. 5. Sketch of the VBK transfer scheme [9, 10]. The coupled chain, containing optical photons (ˆa), phonons (ˆb), magnons ( ˆm) and microwaves (ˆc) generates a two-mode optics-microwave squeezed state σac. Teleportation of a state σ0 consists on performing a displacement in the output microwave σout conditioned on the result of a homodyne detection in the optical part, indicated by the amplitude z.

FIG. 7. Steady state entanglement as a function of op- tomechanical (Cab) and magnon-microwave (Cmc) coopera- tivity, for the proposed disk-based setup. The magnomechan- ical cooperativity was set, based on numerical analysis, to Cmb = 4000. The inlet shows the enhancement factor α as a function of the pump power. The upper scale shows the factor α for three values of Cab. The values of the relevant parameters are given in Table I.

Spintronics.note: The paper is highly relevant as it directly studies the interface between magnons in a YIG disk and cavity photons (microwave/optical) to achieve quantum entanglement and state transfer.
